name: Al Mukaymen City
location: Al-Madinah Al-Munawwarah, Kingdom of Saudi Arabia
description: This project provides consultancy and project management (PMC) services for the Al- Muqeem Municipality project in Madinah. The team oversees all stages from planning to execution and handover, including preparing tender documents, schedules, cost estimates, and risk plans. They monitor contractor performance, ensure quality and safety compliance, manage meetings, review technical submissions, and maintain project documentation. The scope also includes supervision of handover procedures, snag list closure, and final project reports

name: State Security Headquarters
description: The scope of services includes reviewing and studying the project designs, detailed and execution drawings, and overall project plans to ensure compliance with the approved intent and specifications, supervising the on-site execution works, reviewing project budgets and quantity surveying activities, and studying and monitoring the project schedule to ensure timely completion In addition, the role includes evaluating the performance and competence of contractors and suppliers, providing technical opinions and recommendations regarding any proposed modifications, reviewing payment certificates, and reviewing and approving all equipment and materials proposed for the project Regular progress reports shall be prepared and submitted, and participation in

name: Ministry of Hajj and Umrah
description: This 12-month ongoing project, initiated in 2025 by the Ministry of Hajj and Umrah in Saudi Arabia, involves comprehensive consultancy services for supervising projects in Makkah, Madinah, and Jeddah. The scope includes reviewing designs and budgets, supervising execution, assessing contractors and schedules, approving materials and equipment, and participating in the full project handover process

name: Wadi Jazan Resort
description: Inspired by Jazan’s natural beauty, its lush landscapes, waterfalls, and beaches, this project delivers a fully integrated design and execution solution for the Ministry of Water & Electricity, covering architecture, structure, MEP, landscape, interior, environmental services, and complete project management and supervision

name: Half Moon Bay
description: Located in Dammam, KSA, and commissioned by the Municipality of the Eastern Region, this 60,000 feddans project was completed in 2013 with an estimated construction value of 14 billion SAR. It reimagines the bay by integrating land and water to create a distinct identity for the area. Designed to offer a seamless connection between natural and built elements, it provides users with a joyful, immersive experience Services included architecture, landscape, urban planning and design, transportation and mobility studies, environmental and social studies, and civil/structural engineering

name: King Abdullah Suburb in Jazan
description: Completed in 2015, this landscape project in Jazan, Saudi Arabia, was carried out for the Jazan Municipality in joint venture with ACE (Moharram - Bakhoum). It involved providing all technical and professional services for the study and design of the King Abdullah suburb’s landscape

name: Upper Egypt Local Development Program
description: This ongoing project across Minya, Assiut, Sohag, and Qena supports the World Bank and Government of Egypt’s UELDP by enhancing local administration’s capacity for integrated urban and rural development. BECT provides technical support in urban planning and design, including service design guidelines, intervention prioritization, execution supervision for six projects, and capacity building for local departments

name: Madinah Lighting, Electricity & Renewable Energy
description: This ongoing project in Madinah, KSA, commissioned by the Madinah Municipality, involves the rehabilitation and operation of road and district lighting systems, with an estimated construction value of 375 million SAR. Services include supervision, approvals, design reviews, and progress reporting throughout execution

name: Soul Luxury Resort
description: This project involves the development of a luxury resort on the North Coast, featuring 141 villas and 172 buildings designed to the highest standards. Services include reviewing design drawings, monitoring work progress, overseeing the project schedule and cash flow, and ensuring quality and HSE compliance for Eagle Hills Properties and CIB

name: City Gate
description: This project covers the construction of 302 residential villas across 255,000 square meters in New Cairo’s District 3. Services include reviewing the master plan, design drawings, work progress, schedule, cash flow, and ensuring adherence to quality and HSE standards for Bawabat Al Sharq Real Estate Investments and CIB

name: East Town Block 6
description: This ongoing residential project in Cairo for SODIC and CIB involves the construction of 7 housing units, 2 transformers, and a full basement, spanning 9,400 square meters across a high ground floor, three upper floors, and a rooftop. Services include reviewing drawings, monitoring progress, project scheduling, cash flow management, and ensuring quality and HSE compliance

name: Ramses Square Development
description: Winner of 1st prize in an urban design competition, this 930,000-square-meter Ramsis Square project in Downtown Cairo reimagines the space with greenery and pedestrian- friendly features. Commissioned by the Ministry of Culture, it includes architecture, landscape, transportation, environmental and social studies, and urban planning, with an estimated construction value of 2 billion EGP

name: Cairo Historic Panorama
description: The Historic Cairo Panorama project, launched in 2023 with a budget of 9 billion EGP, is a major redevelopment initiative led by the Ministry of Housing. Spanning 36 months, it aims to revitalize the historic district with residential, administrative, and tourism facilities. Services include site surveys, detailed architectural and engineering designs, and full supervision and coordination of execution works

name: CoC2 Center of Excellence
description: This ongoing 2025 project in Maghagha, Minya, focuses on the full architectural design of the CoC2 Center of Excellence as part of Egypt’s national effort to enhance vocational and technical education. The project reimagines an existing industrial secondary school into a modern campus, blending its original architectural character with a new, future-ready design. Services include all design phases, technical documentation, and tendering support

name: Cosmos Village
description: An 810,000 m² educational and cultural destination presenting real-scale models of Egypt’s landmarks through integrated architecture, landscape, interiors, structure, MEP, urban design, project management, and supervision.

name: Local Economic Development (LED) Projects
location: Fayoum, Beni Suef, Luxor, and Aswan, Egypt
description: This ongoing 2024 project supports the Ministry of Local Development in designing and supervising local development projects across Fayoum, Beni Suef, Luxor, and Aswan. With a budget of 480 million Euros and a 2-year timeline, it aims to establish a decentralized governance model and enhance local public service delivery and economic development Services include design, tender documentation, construction supervision, and capacity building for local authorities

name: Port Said Marina Complex
description: This project involved the development of a yacht marina and a tourist complex built over an area of 350 acres in Port Said. The work scope included preparing the master plan, architectural design, and project management. Responsibilities also covered supervising the execution and conducting site inspections to ensure that all construction and tourism- related facilities, including the marina infrastructure, were completed to standard. The project achieved 100% completion

name: Qataf Mixed-Use Complex
description: This 11-month project, completed in 2015 for Qataf Real Estate Development, involved the master planning and conceptual civil design of a 175,000 m² mixed-use complex in Khobar, Saudi Arabia. The scope covered water and wastewater network design, irrigation systems, and road infrastructure, supporting the development of a fully integrated urban environment

name: Cleopatra Mall
description: A 384,000 m² landmark commercial center combining retail, cinemas, hotels, and restaurants through integrated architecture, landscape, engineering, urban design, project management, and site supervision.

name: Al Jawhara Mall
description: This project provides consulting and supervision services for two commercial complexes belonging to the National Housing Company (NHC) in Jeddah. The scope covers preparing and reviewing time schedules and budgets, auditing and approving payment documents, controlling cost, time and quality, reviewing all design and shop drawings, supervising construction on site, inspecting and approving equipment and materials, issuing technical opinions on any changes, preparing periodic reports, and overseeing initial and final handover along with safety plans and recommendations

name: Jeddah Heights Sales Center
description: This project covers consulting and supervision services for a dedicated sales center for the National Housing Company (NHC) in Jeddah. The scope includes preparing work plans to control time, cost, and quality, supervising execution on site, auditing and approving payment documents, and reviewing all detailed and execution drawings It also involves reviewing budgets, monitoring the project schedule, overseeing contractor performance, providing technical opinions on variations, and checking materials and equipment The consultant prepares regular reports, participates in initial and final handover, and develops the safety and security plan with the necessary recommendations

name: Qayati and Al-Ailili Historic Houses on Al-Muizz Street
description: An adaptive-reuse and conservation program for two historic houses on Al-Muizz Street, combining archival research, architectural and structural assessment, restoration planning, infrastructure upgrades, and tender documentation.

name: Restoration of Al-Mahrouqi House and the Facade of Al-Arabi Mosque
description: Restoration of Al-Mahrouqi House and the Al-Arabi Mosque facade through detailed historical, architectural, and structural studies, utility modernization, and complete implementation documentation.

name: Egypt’s National Universities
location: Several cities, Egypt ; Banha, Zagazig, Bani Suif, Helwan, Mansoura, Ganoub El Wadi, El Minya, Assiut, Qanat El Suez, Port Said, Mnufia
description: Completed in 2020 across multiple cities in Egypt, this project for the Ministry of Higher Education involved landscape and urban design for universities. It featured local greenery, courts, pergolas, tents, and food courts to enhance comfort, shade, and the overall user experience. Services included urban planning and landscape design

name: People’s Square
description: This ongoing project in Egypt’s New Administrative Capital involves the urban and landscape design of a major public square for the Egyptian Army. Blending nature with city life, it features diverse plants, open theaters, and large gardens to create vibrant, multifunctional public spaces. Services include landscape, urban planning and design, project management, and site supervision

name: ICity International School
description: A 10-feddan private school designed as a high-quality educational environment, integrating architecture, landscape, interiors, environmental design, structure, MEP, project management, and supervision.

name: Lycee Francais du Caire
description: Currently in progress, this 12,500 m² project in Maadi upgrades the Lycée Français du Caire (LFC) to create a high-quality, engaging educational environment that enriches student life. Services include architecture, landscape, interior design, environmental consulting, structural and MEP works, along with full project management and site supervision

name: Water Supply Networks & GIS Systems
description: This ongoing project in El Baha, KSA, for the Ministry of Water & Electricity involves the design of water and irrigation systems, including networks, pumping stations, and reservoirs, integrated with the city’s GIS. With a construction value of 400 million SAR, BECT provided architecture, structural, MEP, and full project management and site supervision services

name: Aflaj Control Chamber, Pump Station and Irrigation
description: This ongoing irrigation project in El-Ahsa, Saudi Arabia, for the General Organization for Irrigation features a new central station with a 10,000 m³ tank, VFD pumps, and a 58 km irrigation network serving 194 beneficiaries across 80 hectares. Services include Supervision Over Monitoring & Controlling Chamber, Pump Station and Irrigation Networks, design and tender reviews, reporting, schedule and cash flow approvals, material reviews, quantity surveying, and overall project delivery

name: Riyadh Treated Water Re-Usage
description: Completed in 2021, this treated water reuse project in Riyadh supports Saudi Arabia’s national plan to establish 204 sewage treatment plants. Services included civil and structural engineering, MEP, and full project management and site supervision for the Ministry of Water and Electricity

name: 10 Underground Dams in Jazan
description: Completed in 2011, this dam project in the Jazan region of KSA aimed to enhance underground water utilization and aquifer storage in a semi-arid environment prone to drought. Services included structural and civil works, MEP, urban design and planning, and full project management and site supervision for the Ministry of Water and Electricity

name: Bish Water Transmission Pipeline
description: Completed in 2015 for the Ministry of Water and Electricity, this 90 million SAR project involved constructing a water transmission pipeline from Wadi Bish Dam to the Jazan water treatment plant. Delivered in 14 months, it included geotechnical investigations, detailed civil and electromechanical design, concrete element design, value engineering, and tender documentation. Services covered civil/structural works, MEP, project management, and site supervision

name: Wadi Dayqah Dam
description: Completed in 2011, the Wadi Dayqah Dam in Oman was constructed to collect periodic rainfall and stands as the highest dam in the country. Services provided included architecture, structural and civil engineering, and MEP, for the Sultanate of Oman Ministry of Irrigation in collaboration with VINCI

name: El Sawaleh Fakos
description: This project for Sharkia Water & Wastewater Company focuses on upgrading and rehabilitating sewage networks in the Fakous/El Sawaleh cluster, including El Hegagia, Sawada, and El Rawda. Services include architecture, structural and civil engineering, MEP, and full project management and site supervision

name: Baish Substations Package
description: This ongoing project in KSA includes four main substations—Baish, Farsha, Muhaiyl, and Wadi Ben Hashbal—and four extensions: Haqu, Khamis North, Sabt Al Jarah, and Tihama Commissioned by GE and SEC, each substation has an estimated construction value of 85 million SAR. Services cover architecture, structural and civil works, MEP, and technical support

name: South Helwan Substations
description: Completed in 2015 in Helwan, Egypt, this substation project for General Electric (GE) and EETC involved providing architecture, structural and civil engineering, MEP, and technical support services

name: IDP Iraq Substations Package
description: This ongoing project in Iraq involves the detailed civil design of four IDP electric substations for General Electric (GE) and the Iraq Ministry of Electricity, with an estimated construction value of 17 million USD each. Services include architecture, structural and civil engineering, MEP, and technical support

name: POC Baghdad Substations Package
description: This ongoing substation project in Iraq, commissioned by General Electric (GE) in collaboration with the Iraq Ministry of Electricity, involves the detailed civil and MEP design of multiple POC electric substations. Each substation is valued at approximately 50 million USD and plays a key role in enhancing the country’s electrical infrastructure. The scope of work includes comprehensive engineering designs to ensure the substations meet international standards for reliability, safety, and operational efficiency, supporting Iraq’s growing energy demands

name: Balgran Substations Package
description: This ongoing substation project in KSA, led by General Electric (GE) in partnership with the Saudi Electric Company (SEC), includes the development of two main substations—Balgran and Bisha—as well as two extensions in El Arida and Sabya. Each substation is valued at approximately 70 million SAR. The project aims to enhance the national power grid’s capacity and stability through advanced electrical infrastructure and reliable substation design, contributing to the Kingdom’s growing energy needs

name: Zambia Substations Package
description: Completed in 2017, this GIS/AIS substation project in Kafue and Muzuma, Zambia, was delivered for Alstom Grid and ZESCO, with each substation valued at 20 million USD. The project aimed to enhance grid reliability and expand electricity access in the region. Services included architecture, structural and civil engineering, MEP, and technical support for the development of this critical power infrastructure

name: Dammam HVDC Substations
description: Completed in 2008 in Dammam, KSA, this substation project for the Ministry of Water and Electricity in collaboration with Areva (T&D) involved the construction of a back-to-back converters facility for high voltage direct current (HVDC). Valued at 76 million Euros, the project played a key role in strengthening the region’s power transmission infrastructure

name: Ain Shams University Medical City
description: Completed in 2018 for the Egyptian Ministry of Higher Education, this 4 billion EGP project involved the comprehensive design and development of Ain Shams University Medical City in Cairo. It included urban design, site coordination, detailed designs for main axes, facades, squares, gates, and walls, the restoration of Demerdash Palace, and construction of a new ER building and multi-storey garage. Services included architecture, structure, MEP, planning, landscape, tender documents, and full project management and site supervision

name: Lazik Hospital
description: Completed in 2009, this 370 m² specialized Retina Center in Heliopolis was developed for the Retina Consultant Center. The project included conceptual and detailed design, tender documentation, and full architectural, structural, MEP, and project management services

name: Al- Laith Hospital
description: This hospital project in Al-Laith, KSA, was completed in 2011 for the Saudi Ministry of Health, with a construction value of 40 million EGP. Services included architecture, structural and civil engineering, MEP, technical support, and full project management

name: Katameya Hospital
description: Completed in 2019, this 2,400 m² private hospital in New Cairo was developed for Katameya Clinic for Medical Services, with a construction value of 75 million EGP. Designed to optimize space and enhance the experience for both doctors and patients, services included architecture, interior design, landscape, MEP, structural works, and full project management and site supervision

name: Alexandria Police Hospital
description: Completed in 2009 in Alexandria, Egypt, this hospital project for the Ministry of Interior Affairs included full services in architecture, structural and civil engineering, MEP, technical support, and project management

name: Cairo Scan
description: Completed in 2008 for a private sector client in Egypt, this medical establishment project included comprehensive services in architecture, structural and civil engineering, MEP, technical support, and project management

name: Orouba Hospital
description: Completed in 2008 for a private sector client in Egypt, this hospital project involved full architectural, structural, civil, MEP, and technical support services, along with complete project management

name: NAT Office Complex
description: Completed in 2013, the 34,000 m² headquarters for the National Authority for Tunnels (NAT) in 6th of October City reflects the theme of connection, echoing NAT’s role in linking Cairo through metro lines. The complex consists of five four-story office buildings connected by elevated bridges, with underground parking. Services included architecture, landscape, interior design, structure, MEP, and project management

name: GIG Headquarters
description: Completed in 2021, the GIG headquarters in New Cairo spans six floors with two underground levels, each covering 4,500 m². Designed to reflect a modern corporate identity, the 80 million EGP project included architecture, structural and MEP works, landscape, interior design, environmental services, and full project management and site supervision for Arab Misr Insurance Group (GIG)

name: World Bank Headquarters
description: Completed in 2018, the World Bank Group’s 1,300 m² Cairo headquarters at Nile City Towers was designed to accommodate 80 employees while reflecting the institution’s global image BECT provided architecture, interior design, environmental services, MEP, and full project management and site supervision, with a construction value of 15 million EGP

name: Alstom Headquarters
description: Completed in 2019, Alstom Transport’s headquarters in Cairo’s 5th Settlement spans two floors of 1,500 m² each, with a featured 200 m² office in Cairo Festival City. Designed to enhance project communication and administration, the 30 million EGP project included architecture, interior design, MEP, environmental services, and full project management and site supervision

name: IFAO New Library
description: Currently in progress, the IFAO extension in Downtown Cairo spans 2,400 m² and features a library for rare archaeological documents, along with offices and administrative spaces Designed to integrate with the existing structure while expressing a modern identity, services include architecture, structural and civil engineering, MEP, environmental consultancy, and full project management and site supervision

name: WFP Regional Office
description: Architectural and MEP services for the World Food Programme regional office in Downtown Cairo.

name: Mission Economique
description: A diplomatic and cultural workplace in Zamalek delivered through architecture, landscape, interiors, structural and civil engineering, MEP, project management, and site supervision.

name: Institut Français D’Egypte
description: A comprehensive cultural project for the Institut Français d’Égypte, covering architecture, landscape, interiors, structural and civil works, MEP, project management, and supervision.

name: Royal Norwegian Embassy
description: A diplomatic office project in Zamalek combining architecture, interiors, landscape, structural and civil engineering, MEP, project management, and supervision.

name: Utopia Pharmaceutical Factory
description: Completed in 2022, this 18,296 m² pharmaceutical factory for Utopia Pharmaceuticals in 10th of Ramadan was built using advanced industry technologies. It includes offices, administrative areas, labs, and technical zones. Services covered architecture, interior design, landscape, environmental, structural, MEP, and full project management and site supervision, with a construction value of 320 million EGP

name: Rehabilitation and Construction of Factories in Menya
description: This ongoing 500 million EGP project in Menya involves the rehabilitation and construction of Upper Egypt for Spinning and Weaving Co. factories. Services include preparing detailed designs and tender documents, supervising works, monitoring quality and HSE compliance, approving materials and work receipts, and ensuring cost control in line with the project schedule

name: Rehabilitation and Construction of Factories in Dakahlya
description: This ongoing 700 million EGP project in Mansoura, Dakahlya, involves the rehabilitation and construction of Detex Co. for Spinning & Weaving factories. Services include reviewing detailed and execution drawings (architectural, structural, electrical, and mechanical), supervising works, monitoring quality and HSE compliance, approving materials and work receipts, and controlling costs according to schedule

name: Factories in Mahalla El Kobra
description: This ongoing project in Mahalla El Kobra involves constructing new textile, dyeing, and preparation factories for Misr Spinning & Weaving Co., along with external networks and service buildings. Services include design review, supervision, quality and HSE compliance, material approval, and cost control, all within the project schedule

name: New Cotton Gins
location: Zagazig, Kafr ElZayat, Kafr ElDawar, Egypt
description: A multi-location cotton-gin program across Zagazig, Kafr El Zayat, and Kafr El Dawar, supporting Egypt’s industrial modernization through coordinated design review, supervision, quality, safety, and cost control.

name: Nestle Production Building
description: Renovation of a Nestlé production building in Kalioub through integrated architecture, landscape, interiors, structural and civil engineering, MEP, project management, and supervision.

name: Schneider Factory
description: Completed in 2008, this 27,000 m² private factory in Badr City was developed for Schneider Electric, reflecting the company’s vision for a more electric, decarbonized, decentralized, and digitized future. Services included architecture, landscape, MEP, structural works, and full project management and site supervision

name: Bel Egypt Office and Factory
description: An integrated office and factory project for Bel Egypt in 10th of Ramadan, covering architecture, landscape, interiors, structural and civil engineering, MEP, project management, and supervision.

name: Greater Cairo MetroLine 1 Projects
description: Projects Location: Egypt Date of Accomplishment: 2016 Supervision of Kozzika Metro Workshop This project for the National Authority for Tunnels (NAT) involved the supervision of the Kozzika Metro Workshop, with an estimated construction value of 80 million Euros. Services included architecture, structural and civil works, MEP, project management, and full site supervision Construction & Commissioning of 20 Trains Completed in 2016 for NAT, this 120 million Euro project covered the construction and commissioning of 20 metro trains Services provided included MEP, project management, and site supervision

name: Greater Cairo Metro Line 2 Projects
description: Projects Location: Egypt Date of Accomplishment: 2016 Metro Stations – Individual Projects Projects include the construction of El Monib Station (90 million EGP), Sakiat Mekky Station (80 million EGP), and El Attaba Station (150 million EGP) for the National Authority for Tunnels (NAT) in partnership with VINCI Services provided included architecture, structure/civil, MEP, project management, and site supervision Metro Stations – Clustered Developments This cluster includes Behoos and Massara Stations (120 million EGP each), along with El Mazalat, Giza, and Cairo University Stations (combined value of 285 million EGP) All projects were delivered for NAT and involved full multidisciplinary services from design through to site supervision

name: Greater Cairo MetroLine 3 Projects
description: Projects Location: Egypt Date of Accomplishment: 2016 Workshops and Industrial Buildings – Cairo Metro Line 3, Abbassia Station This project for the National Authority for Tunnels (NAT), in collaboration with SYSTRA & ACE, involved the design of workshops and industrial buildings for Cairo Metro Line 3 at Abbassia Station. With an estimated construction value of 650 million EGP, services included architecture, structural and civil engineering, and MEP

name: Port Said Tunnels
description: Completed in 2015 for the National Authority for Tunnels, this 4.5 billion EGP project in Port Said involved the design of two road tunnels totaling 4 km under the Suez Canal, along with a feasibility study for a third railway tunnel. Services included architecture, structural and civil engineering, MEP, urban planning, environmental and social studies, and transportation and mobility studies

name: Al-Azhar Tunnels
description: A Cairo tunnel project for the National Authority for Tunnels, providing architecture, civil and structural engineering, MEP, project management, and site supervision.

name: Swing Bridge
description: A swing bridge in Damietta for the National Authority for Roads & Bridges, delivered through architecture, civil and structural engineering, MEP, project management, and supervision.
